Product Technology

Clinical Technology — The FreeStride™ System

Each component addresses a specific clinical risk factor. The following features are referenced in the clinical monograph (Document AIRSTEP-CM-2025-10) and validated through independent laboratory pedography.

Podiatric-Informed Design
Wider toe box, higher bridge & rocker sole for natural alignment and better posture.
Triple Layer Footbed
Absorbs impact and supports all-day wear. Removable for custom orthotics.
Seam-Free Inner Fit
Stitched inside-out to eliminate internal pressure points — critical for diabetic feet.
Phylon Midsole + TPU Stabiliser
Responsive cushioning with built-in anti-twist support for safer, more confident movement.
About Airstep

Clinical Development & Regulatory Status

Airstep was developed in collaboration with South African podiatrists, orthopaedic specialists, and endocrinologists to address a defined clinical need: patients with complex foot pathology lacked a medically validated, locally manufactured device meeting formal prescribing requirements.

Each unit is manufactured in South Africa to export-grade standards with full production traceability. The upper is crafted from genuine ISO certified cow suede — breathable, durable, and clinically appropriate for sensitive and high-risk feet.

The FreeStride™ last is a registered design — built on 50+ years of combined South African podiatric and German orthopaedic shoemaking expertise. The engineered sole architecture activates both the foot and calf muscle venous pumps, promoting venous and arterial blood circulation return — critical for patients with vascular compromise.

As a Class A Medical Device with GMDN registration, NAPPI coding, and SAMED membership, Airstep sits firmly within formal clinical prescribing pathways — not the grey zone of wellness or comfort footwear.

Clinical Indications

Clinical Indications

Airstep is indicated for patients presenting with the following pathologies. Appropriate for prescription across podiatry, endocrinology, orthotics & prosthetics, physiotherapy, rheumatology, and general practice.

Diabetic Foot
Arthritis
Neuropathy
Structural Deformity
Post-Surgical
Oedema
Plantar Fasciitis
Post-Prosthetic
Fat Pad Atrophy
—
01
FreeStride™ Anatomical Last — Registered Design
Registered design based on 50+ years of combined podiatric and orthopaedic shoemaking research. In-flare angle of no more than 3 degrees — counteracting in-shoe pressure on the 4th and 5th toes caused by conventional 6-degree lasts.
02
Triple-Layer Removable Insole
Moulded polyurethane top layer with two open-cell foam layers of different densities. Removable to accommodate custom orthotics without compromising cushioning.
03
Extra Depth + up to 4E width
Built-in extra depth protects vulnerable dorsal toe aspects. Up to 4E width accommodates structural deformities, oedema, and wide prescription insoles.
04
Validated Pressure Offloading
German laboratory pedography confirms even plantar pressure with no high-risk zones. Prototype trials with diabetic adults showed reduced shear and enhanced circulation.
05
Engineered Gait Mechanics
55-degree minimum forefoot flexion ensures the shoe bends at the MTPJs. Engineered toe spring and minimal heel-to-toe drop promote natural gait and reduce fall risk.
06
Minimal-Seam Construction & Genuine Leather Upper
ISO certified cow suede upper — breathable, naturally moisture-wicking, and gentle on fragile skin. Reversed lining, padded tongue, and double-walled eyelets to shield skin on the inside of the shoe. Eliminates internal friction, reducing callus and ulcer risk.
07
Sole Architecture
Slip-resistant rubber outer sole with Phylon midsole for cushioning and torsional control. Calculated toe spring encourages natural rolling motion. Ankle flexion during gait activates the calf muscle pump, returning venous blood from the feet to the heart.
08
Venous & Arterial Circulation
Sole architecture and engineered forefoot flexion activate both the foot and calf muscle venous pumps, promoting both venous and arterial blood circulation return — clinically significant for patients with peripheral vascular disease or vascular compromise secondary to diabetes.
Clinical & Technical Monograph

Document AIRSTEP-CM-2025-10

Full prescribing information for healthcare professionals. Prepared November 2025. Reviewed by the Airstep Clinical Team, Telmo International Standards Group.

Document ID: AIRSTEP-CM-2025-10 · 7 November 2025
Clinical & Technical Monograph: Airstep Medical Footwear
Prepared ForPodiatrists, Endocrinologists, GPs, Orthotists
SubjectMedical-Grade Athleisure Footwear
ClassificationClass A Medical Device
1.0Introduction

Airstep is a medical-grade athleisure footwear system developed in South Africa, representing a synthesis of podiatric expertise and master shoemaking. It is engineered to address the complex biomechanical and vascular needs of individuals with sensitive feet, particularly those at high risk for ulceration, pressure-related injuries, and gait instability.

The design objective is to provide a therapeutic shoe that combines medical-grade features — enhanced depth, pressure-offloading insoles, and anatomical lasts — with contemporary athletic styling, thereby improving patient compliance and clinical outcomes.

2.0Indications for Use
  • Diabetes Mellitus: Ambulatory patients requiring preventative footwear to minimise pressure and shear, reducing the risk of diabetic foot ulcers (DFUs).
  • Arthritis (Rheumatoid, Osteoarthritis): To accommodate arthritic toe deformities and reduce joint stress during ambulation.
  • Structural Deformities: Including Hallux Abducto Valgus (bunions) and digital deformities such as hammer toes and claw toes.
  • Plantar Fat Pad Atrophy: Providing supplementary cushioning to compensate for diminished natural padding.
  • General Sensitive Feet: Individuals experiencing foot pain from extended standing or vascular insufficiency.
3.0Clinical Problem Addressed
  1. Lacking Volumetric Space: Insufficient depth and width to accommodate foot deformities or prescription orthotics.
  2. Creating High-Pressure Zones: Poorly designed lasts and thin foam liners that fail to distribute plantar pressure evenly.
  3. Inducing Friction and Shear: Internal seams and improper fit contribute to shear stress — a key factor in blister and ulcer formation.
  4. Impeding Natural Biomechanics: Incorrect flexion points (not matching the MTPJs) and heel-to-toe drops can alter natural gait.
  5. Corns & Calluses: Formed as the protective response to repeated pressure or friction on the heels, balls, and toes of the feet.
4.0Core Technology and Key Design Features
4.1 FreeStride™ Anatomical Last
  • Reduced In-flare: Reduces torsional pressure on the sides of the feet compared to a conventional 6-degree last.
  • Anatomical Shape: Allows the foot to sit naturally, providing alignment support and pressure management.
4.2 Key Biomechanical & Accommodative Features
Performance FeatureSpecificationClinical Benefit
Volumetric FitUp to 4E width fittingAccommodates wider foot types and structural deformities without compression
Enhanced DepthBuilt-in extra depth throughoutProtects vulnerable dorsal toe aspects; allows custom or prescription orthotics
Insole SystemTriple-layer removable insoles (moulded PU top + two open-cell foam layers)Effective shock absorption and pressure distribution; removable for orthotics
Internal ConstructionReversed lining; padded tongue; double-walled eyeletsMinimises friction, protecting skin integrity and reducing ulcer risk
Gait MechanicsEngineered toe spring; 55-degree minimum forefoot flexionPromotes natural gait cycle; shoe flexes correctly at MTPJs
StabilityMinimal heel-to-toe drop; rubber solesOptimises weight distribution; reduces fall risk
Upper MaterialGenuine leather — ISO certified cow suedeBreathable, durable, naturally moisture-wicking; reduces friction and maceration risk in high-risk feet
ConstructionFlexible Strobel stitchingProvides flexibility that adapts to the foot's motion
5.0Biomechanical Mechanism of Action
  1. Pressure and Shear Reduction: The FreeStride™ last, minimal-seam lining, and triple-layer insoles provide even plantar pressure distribution — minimising peak pressure under metatarsal heads and reducing friction, directly mitigating the primary catalysts for DFUs.
  2. Circulatory Enhancement: Engineered sole architecture activates the foot and calf muscle venous pumps, promoting blood circulation — critical for patients with vascular compromise.
  3. Accommodation and Protection: Enhanced depth and up to 4E width prevent compression of deformities such as hammer toes and arthritic joints.
6.0Clinical Evidence and Validation
Research Basis — Thompson (2021)
Identified that conventional lasts and flexion points often mismatch the MTPJs, causing focused pressure and poor blood flow. FreeStride™ was developed to correct this over 20+ years of foot measurement research.
In-Shoe Pressure Measurements
Pedography analysis by a German laboratory confirmed even pressure distribution with no high-pressure zones — validating Airstep's efficacy in offloading high-risk areas.
Prototype Trials
Monitored wear trials with non-neuropathic diabetic adults demonstrated reduced plantar shear stress and vertical pressure, and enhanced blood circulation.
7.0Regulatory Status and Endorsements
️
Class A Medical Device
Registered globally on the GMDN register as a Class A Medical Device
Telmo Endorsed
Recognised by Telmo — international consortium of podiatrists and scientists who set diabetic footwear standards
NAPPI Coded — SA
NAPPI coding facilitates prescription and medical aid reimbursement in South Africa
8.0Conclusion

Airstep medical-grade footwear provides a clinically validated solution for managing foot health in high-risk patient populations. By integrating the anatomically precise FreeStride™ last with features designed to reduce pressure, minimise shear, and promote circulation, it assists in the prevention of foot complications associated with diabetes, arthritis, and foot risk conditions.

Clinical Presence

Congresses & Professional Engagements

Airstep has been presented to and engaged with the South African clinical community at the following professional congresses.

Podiatry

PASA Congress

Podiatry Association of South Africa
South Africa · 2025

Airstep was presented at the PASA annual congress, engaging directly with South Africa's podiatric community. Clinical features, the FreeStride™ system, and NAPPI coding were demonstrated to practicing podiatrists across the country.

Podiatry Diabetic Foot FreeStride™
Endocrinology & Diabetes

Diabetes Alliance Congress

Airstep was exhibited at the Diabetes Alliance Congress, placing the device directly in front of endocrinologists, diabetologists, and GPs managing patients with diabetic foot complications. NAPPI coding and PMB eligibility were central to clinical discussions.

For enquiries about clinical presentations or congress documentation, contact us on 074 906 0388 or rory@airstepfootwear.com

Prescribing Partnership

For Prescribing Clinicians

Airstep is designed to integrate cleanly into a clinical prescribing workflow. The information below covers how to dispense, code, and support patients through the medical aid claims process.

Dispensing & Ordering

In-practice dispensing, same appointment

Airstep can be prescribed, fitted, and dispensed in a single appointment. No minimum order. Stock on consignment or order per patient — your preference.

Trade accounts are set up within one business day. All sizes UK 2–13, 4E width, held in stock in Johannesburg and Cape Town with same-day despatch.

NAPPI Coding & Medical Aid

Fully coded. PMB claimable.

Airstep carries a registered NAPPI code and qualifies under PMB Circular 13/2024 as a therapeutic orthotic device for diabetic foot management.

Prescribing documentation and clinical support materials are available on request.

Clinical Documentation

Full prescribing support available

Available on request: clinical monograph, peer-reviewed pressure distribution data, motivational letter templates, GMDN registration documentation, and NAPPI coding guidance.

Samples available for clinical evaluation prior to stocking. Airstep's extra-depth construction accommodates custom orthotic insoles — the shoe is designed with sufficient internal volume to fit full-length custom orthotics without compression. Orthotists working with Sensor Medica software can search for the Airstep last template directly within the platform to design patient-specific insoles.

Regulatory Status

Class A medical device

Manufactured under ISO 9001:2015 quality management standards. GMDN registered. NAPPI coded. SAHPRA-compliant Class A medical device classification for therapeutic footwear.

Data Privacy

POPIA Compliance Notice

Tradeshoe (Pty) Ltd (Reg: 2018/051444/07) collects and processes personal information in accordance with the Protection of Personal Information Act, 4 of 2013 (POPIA). Any personal information submitted through this website is used solely for the purpose of fulfilling your request — whether that is processing a trade account application, responding to a clinical enquiry, or providing prescribing documentation.

For more information on your rights under POPIA, visit the Information Regulator of South Africa at www.justice.gov.za/inforeg. To lodge a complaint with the Regulator: complaints.IR@justice.gov.za.
